text.source_text: इतश्च शारीरादन्यो मनोमयत्वादिगुणः ; यस्माच्छब्दविशेषो भवति समानप्रकरणे श्रुत्यन्तरे — ‘ यथा व्रीहिर्वा यवो वा श्यामाको वा श्यामाकतण्डुलो वैवमयमन्तरात्मन्पुरुषो हिरण्मयः ’ (श. ब्रा. १० । ६ । ३ । २) इति । शारीरस्यात्मनो यः शब्दोऽभिधायकः सप्तम्यन्तः — अन्तरात्मन्निति ; तस्माद्विशिष्टोऽन्यः प्रथमान्तः पुरुषशब्दो मनोमयत्वादिविशिष्टस्यात्मनोऽभिधायकः । तस्मात्तयोर्भेदोऽधिगम्यते ॥ ५ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca śārīrādanyo manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yasmācchabdaviśeṣo bhavati samānaprakaraṇe śrutyantare — ‘ yathā vrīhirvā yavo vā śyāmāko vā śyāmākataṇḍulo vaivamayamantarātmanpuruṣo hiraṇmayaḥ ’ (śa. brā. 10 | 6 | 3 | 2) iti | śārīrasyātmano yaḥ śabdo'bhidhāyakaḥ saptamyantaḥ — antarātmanniti ; tasmādviśiṣṭo'nyaḥ prathamāntaḥ puruṣaśabdo manomayatvādiviśiṣṭasyātmano'bhidhāyakaḥ | tasmāttayorbhedo'dhigamyate || 5 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the one with mind-made and related attributes is other than the embodied self: there is a specific wording in a parallel passage of the same topic - 'as a grain of rice or barley... this golden person in the inner self.' The word denoting the embodied self appears in locative form ('in the inner self'), while a distinct nominative word 'person' denotes another self qualified by mind-made and related attributes. Thus, difference between the two is understood. (5)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.5 uses grammatical distinction (saptami vs prathama reference) in parallel shruti to infer bheda between sharira-jiva locus and worshipped purusha.
translation.interpretive_note: Grammatical case contrast is treated as interpretive evidence.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit sharira/jiva/upadhi precision in records 1.2.007-1.2.014.
